title: Gouinia brandegeei
taxonomy: Gouinia brandegeei is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is a member of the tribe Panicoideae, which is a large group of grasses. The genus Gouinia is a part of the subfamily Panicoideae, and it is primarily found in Central and South America. intro: Gouinia brandegeei, also known as Tanglehead Grass, is a perennial grass native to the Americas, characterized by its distinctive tangled growth habit and small, delicate flowers. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it beneficial for wound healing and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The bioactive constituents of Gouinia brandegeei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties.
